title: AC Not Turning On | Tampa, FL
hero: ‘Not turning on’ splits into permission (stats, safeties, float) versus power path (breaker, disconnect, contactor) versus load faults (short, locked rotor). Random resets destroy evidence.
summary_30s: Prove 24 V call at the outdoor board, then line voltage at the disconnect, then contactor pull-in and compressor/fan attempt. Indoor blower-only with dead outdoor is not ‘random’—it is a controlled branch map.

quick_checks.label: Thermostat and mode
quick_checks.action: COOL or FAN ON as intended; setpoint; batteries or blank stat addressed.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Chasing outdoor electrical on a stat schedule fault wastes time and parts.

quick_checks.label: Breaker and disconnect
quick_checks.action: HVAC breaker set; pull disconnect fully seated; no melted stabs.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Repeated resets on a short can arc damage the panel and outdoor whip.

quick_checks.label: Condensate float
quick_checks.action: If wired in series with R, open float reads as dead outdoor.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Bypassing float without fixing drain invites pan overflow and hidden water damage.

quick_checks.label: Contactor pull
quick_checks.action: With safe access, verify 24 V coil and contacts pull when stat calls.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Live troubleshooting without training risks shock on wet condenser pads.

quick_checks.label: Audible compressor attempt
quick_checks.action: Listen for hum with no spin (cap/fan) versus silent open circuit.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Ignoring locked rotor signatures burns contactor and lugs.

safe_diy: Reset breaker once if no warmth, odor, or buzz at panel.
Re-seat disconnect after power confirmed off.
Replace stat batteries; verify mode and schedule.
Clear visible primary drain blockage if float tripped.
call_pro_if: Burning smell, hot breaker, or buzzing panel.
Contactor chatters or welds; uncertain 240 V measurement.
Compressor hums, draws high amps, or trips internal—needs megohm and start assist diagnosis.
Any aluminum wiring or insurance-critical panel work.

nodes.label: No cooling; confirm user symptom indoor vs outdoor
24 V present at outdoor Y/O common path
Stat, float, or low-voltage wiring repair
Contactor, cap, or fan motor branch

flow_explanations.A: Split indoor vs outdoor failure before parts.
flow_explanations.B: No blower often stat, door switch, or air handler control power—not outdoor cap.
flow_explanations.C: Missing Y is frequently float, wire, or stat installer error.
flow_explanations.D: Line-side first after low voltage proven—disconnect and breaker discipline.
flow_explanations.E: Low-voltage and supply permission fixes stay in controls lane.
flow_explanations.F: Contactor and PSC cap failures are common; verify coil volts under load.
flow_explanations.G: Compressor electrical is pro-only with proper meters and lockout.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Overcurrent, loose stab, storm, or human error opens line side.
top_causes.risk: Repeated reset without locating fault damages equipment and panel.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Backed pan opens R path to outdoor call.
top_causes.risk: Bypass without drain fix floods ceilings.

top_causes.cause: Failed contactor or 24 V coil path
top_causes.why_it_happens: Pitted contacts or weak coil drops both fan and compressor.
top_causes.risk: Single-phasing and lug heat if contacts weld intermittently.

top_causes.cause: Bad run capacitor or fan motor
top_causes.why_it_happens: PSC fan or compressor assist fails; unit appears dead.
top_causes.risk: Wrong cap MFD rating fries motor windings.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Ground or winding fault trips breaker or internal.
top_causes.risk: Fire and shock if forced with resets.

repair_matrix.fix: Locate fault; repair short; breaker correct size
Clear drain; repair interlock; stat reprogram
Test cap; replace motor or cap as spec

repair_matrix.cause: Weak coil or low voltage drop
repair_matrix.fix: Replace contactor; fix undersized common wire
Isolation test; compressor or whip repair

how_system_works: Split AC needs line power at the outdoor unit, a pulled contactor to pass L1/L2, and a working start/run network for PSC motors. The thermostat only requests cooling; safeties and interlocks must permit 24 V to the outdoor board.

repair_if: Localized wiring, float, contactor, cap, or fan motor failure with sound compressor
Breaker issue corrected with verified amp draw
replace_if: Aged system with stacked electrical failures

local_context: Tampa afternoon storms and humidity corrode outdoor connections; ants in contactor are common after wet seasons.
field_insight: If indoor runs and outdoor never pulls in, I prove Y before I touch capacitors—half the ‘dead’ units are float or stat.
maintenance: Annual electrical torque check on outdoor lugs in coastal humidity
Surge history note on contactor pitting
cost_pressure: Emergency no-cool calls stack after outages; after-hours and wet attic access add labor.
decision_moment: One breaker reset only—if it trips again, stop and call; that is a fault signature not nuisance.
cta_primary: Get a same-day electrical and controls diagnosis before repeated resets damage the panel.
cta_secondary: Hot breaker, smell, or welded contactor noise—shut off and call immediately.
stop_diy: Do not file contactor contacts while energized; do not upsize breakers; do not bypass safeties to get cooling tonight.
